description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the custom Ruby image (${CI_REGISTRY}/gitlab-org/gitlab-build-images/ci/debian-${DEBIAN_VERSION}-slim-ruby-${RUBY_VERSION}:rubygems-${RUBYGEMS_VERSION}-git-${GIT_VERSION}) with a default before_script of `bundle install` and a cache of `vendor/ruby` keyed by Gemfile.lock and gitlab-qa.gemspec (pull policy). Add a `cache-gems` job in the hidden `.pre` stage that runs `echo "Cached gems!"`, caches `vendor/ruby` with pull‑push policy, and is triggered only when Gemfile.lock or .gitlab/ci/versions.yml change on the default branch or a merge request. Add a `danger-review` job that optionally needs `cache-gems`, inherits the default image and before_script, and executes the included danger‑review component. Add an `rspec` job in the `test` stage that optionally needs `cache-gems`, runs `bundle exec rspec --force-color`, and publishes a Cobertura coverage report from `coverage/coverage.xml`; also add a `rubocop` job in the `check` stage that optionally needs `cache-gems` and runs `bundle exec rubocop --color`. Finally, add a `test-on-omnibus-env` job in `.pre` using an Alpine image that runs `scripts/build-package-and-test-env`, outputs a dotenv artifact `build.env`, and sets `UPDATE_QA_CACHE` based on file changes, and a `test-on-omnibus` job in the `qa` stage that depends on `test-on-omnibus-env`, triggers the `.gitlab/ci/omnibus.gitlab-ci.yml` pipeline with forwarded variables, runs manually on merge requests and automatically on the default or stable branches, and defines the required QA environment variables.
